verdict.answer_first: B opens with the direct mechanism: "Both languages free memory automatically by tracking reachability"; A is also direct but starts more generally with "Garbage collection (GC) is automatic memory management."
verdict.decisiveness: B gives a clearer single framing for the answer with "The core algorithm: mark-and-sweep" and then separates JavaScript and Go; A adds more implementation detail and optional tangents.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to use right now because it explains reachability, mark-and-sweep, V8 generational GC, Go concurrent GC, and the main trade-off without burying the core answer in extra detail.
